name: ANFX + Midnight Smart-Money Shakuni Strategy
summary: A liquidity-first Smart Money strategy combining ANFX LSBR execution with Shakuni trap/opening-range sweep logic. It trades after liquidity is collected, structure confirms, and price retests a valid supply/demand zone or imbalance.

core_models.name: LSBR
steps: Identify equal highs/lows, obvious support/resistance, range edges, session highs/lows, or other liquidity pools.
Wait for the sweep or liquidity grab; the sweep itself is not the entry.
Require displacement, rejection, BOS, MSS, or CHOCH in the intended direction.
Wait for a retest into a valid order block, FVG, supply/demand zone, or refined lower-timeframe zone.
Set stop loss beyond the sweep or zone extreme and target the next opposing liquidity pool or supply/demand zone.

core_models.name: Shakuni Trap
steps: Mark the midnight/opening range and obvious swing highs/lows.
A sweep above the range or swing high is bearish only after rejection and bearish structure confirmation.
A sweep below the range or swing low is bullish only after rejection and bullish structure confirmation.
Do not chase the first spike; wait for follow-through and confirmation pullback.
Use strict risk, usually around 1 percent of account capital, with a minimum practical 1:2 risk/reward.

strict_rules: BUY requires sell-side liquidity swept below equal lows/support, bullish MSS/BOS/CHOCH or displacement, and price returning to demand/order block/FVG.
SELL requires buy-side liquidity swept above equal highs/resistance, bearish MSS/BOS/CHOCH or displacement, and price returning to supply/order block/FVG.
No liquidity sweep means no high-quality trade.
No structure confirmation means no trade.
No valid POI retest means no trade.
Avoid weak zones: messy base, slow departure, multiple retests, counter-HTF direction, or strong opposite momentum.
HTF trend is the filter and zone is the trigger.
If setup quality or capital risk is unclear, return NO_TRADE instead of forcing a signal.
zone_validation: Demand zones are Rally-Base-Rally or Drop-Base-Rally origins with strong bullish departure.
Supply zones are Drop-Base-Drop or Rally-Base-Drop origins with strong bearish departure.
Strong zones show explosive departure, little time in base, clear imbalance, and ideally a sweep before departure.
Refine large zones with body-to-body range or the sweep wick extreme to improve risk/reward.
Fresh zones are preferred; zones weaken after repeated touches.
risk_rules: Be honest with the user about capital and margin; do not suggest a trade if account risk is not acceptable.
Prefer 0.5 percent to 1 percent risk per trade unless the user explicitly configured otherwise.
Never open or recommend a demo trade without numeric entry, stop loss, take profit, and risk/reward.
Stop loss belongs beyond the sweep wick or protected zone extreme.
Take profit should target the next logical opposing liquidity pool, supply/demand zone, or at least 1:2 risk/reward.
Backtest or strategy logic does not guarantee profit; market conditions and discipline still matter.

response_contract.reasoning: one clear sentence explaining setup, rejection reason, and capital/risk suitability
